Finding: Selected CUDA 9/XPU 0/CPU 0 could show stale CUDA 6/XPU 2/CPU 1 distribution hint
Resolution / Remaining Boundary: Selected routing now derives from live controls; recommendation is separate and only applied explicitly.

Finding: CPU actor workers label conflated host process cap with routing share units
Resolution / Remaining Boundary: Separated CPU rollout process cap from CUDA/XPU/CPU equivalent share-planner units.

Finding: GUI protected routing could drift from runtime rebinding semantics
Resolution / Remaining Boundary: Shared protected_rollout_shares() is used by reporting and runtime competitive branch binding.

Finding: 24-epoch competitive run displayed 0% and only epochs [2]
Resolution / Remaining Boundary: Target-aware competitive_progress_snapshot reports session/cumulative branch progress and real percentage.
Reports last exact-safe checkpoint and next exact-safe boundary separately.

Finding: Compute status could imply episode-routing count was worker/process count
Resolution / Remaining Boundary: Status explicitly labels counts as EPISODES and planner values as equivalent units.

Finding: CUDA 100% could be interpreted as 100% of all program compute on GPU
Resolution / Remaining Boundary: UI explicitly states percentages are eligible rollout routing; synthetic CALO environment remains host-side in Stage A.

Finding: Synthetic CALO training environment remains substantially CPU/NumPy host-side
Resolution / Remaining Boundary: No GPU-resident environment rewrite is claimed in v6.3 Stage A.

Finding: Normal GUI policy training supplies no real ORPD development cases
Resolution / Remaining Boundary: development_cases remains empty in normal GUI training; accelerator-native ORPD development evaluator therefore has no such workload.

Finding: Windows Task Manager 3D engine can show 0% while CUDA/Compute engine differs
Resolution / Remaining Boundary: In-app runtime assignment is authoritative for scheduling; physical CUDA utilization still requires correct telemetry/engine selection.
